1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

Both custom targets in `IncrementalBroken.csproj` will **always re-run** because they lack `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, which MSBuild uses to determine if a target is up-to-date.

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6)
- **Problem:** Writes a file containing `DateTime.Now` on every build. Since the timestamp changes every second, this always produces a different file, which in turn invalidates `CoreCompile` — forcing a full recompilation even when no source changed.
- **Fix:** This pattern is inherently non-incremental. If you truly need it, gate it behind a condition or move it to a target that doesn't feed into compilation. Otherwise, remove it.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13)
- **Problem:** No `Inputs`/`Outputs` declared, so MSBuild can never skip it. It runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and rewrites `git-hash.g.cs` every time — even when the hash hasn't changed — which also invalidates compilation.
- **Fix:** Add `Inputs` (e.g., `.git/HEAD` and `.git/refs/heads/**`) and `Outputs` (the generated `.cs` file). Additionally, compare the new hash to the existing file content before overwriting to avoid a content-change that triggers recompilation.

### Summary

| Target | Root Cause | Effect |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Always writes a new value (timestamp) | Forces recompile every build |
| `EmbedGitHash` | No Inputs/Outputs; unconditionally overwrites file | Forces recompile every build |

Both targets cause `CoreCompile` to re-run on every build regardless of source code changes, completely defeating incremental builds.
